description:
You are a network engineer who has just mounted a colleague’s
    “remote-share” onto the local workstation at
    /home/user/remote_share/site_backup.  
    Your goal is to prove that the workstation really can reach the
    “remote” location and to synchronise a small set of router
    configuration files.

    The workstation already contains three reference configuration
    files in
        /home/user/source_configs/
            router1.conf
            router2.conf
            router3.conf

    An (initially empty) destination directory that represents the
    remote host is present at
        /home/user/remote_share/site_backup/

    A writable logging directory exists at
        /home/user/logs/

    Perform the following three subtasks from the terminal.  
    Use only a *very small* sequence of sensible Linux commands (no
    more than 2-3 commands per subtask is expected).

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    1) Connectivity verification  
       • Send exactly three (3) ICMP echo-requests to 127.0.0.1.  
       • Redirect the **entire** command output to the file  
         /home/user/logs/connectivity_test.log  
       • Do not prepend or append any extra commentary lines.

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    2) Dry-run synchronisation  
       • Using rsync, perform a *dry run* that would synchronise
         /home/user/source_configs/  ➜  /home/user/remote_share/site_backup/  
       • Mandatory rsync options:  
           -a  --delete  --dry-run  --out-format="%n"  
         (the --out-format option makes rsync print *only* the names of
         files that would be transferred – nothing else).  
       • Redirect STDOUT **only** to  
         /home/user/logs/rsync_dryrun.log  
       • After the command, that log file **must contain exactly three
         lines**, one per file, in this precise order:  
            router1.conf  
            router2.conf  
            router3.conf

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    3) Live synchronisation  
       • Run a second rsync that actually synchronises the same two
         directories.  
       • Use the same options as above but **omit --dry-run** so the
         copy really happens.  
       • Again redirect STDOUT to  
         /home/user/logs/rsync_run.log  
       • When finished, rsync_run.log must also contain the same three
         lines (router1.conf, router2.conf, router3.conf – one per
         line, in that order).

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    Completion criteria (checked automatically)
       1. /home/user/logs/connectivity_test.log exists and contains the
          standard ping output for *exactly* three echo-requests.  
       2. /home/user/logs/rsync_dryrun.log exists and **only** contains
          the three filenames in the order specified.  
       3. /home/user/logs/rsync_run.log exists and **only** contains
          the same three filenames in the same order.  
       4. After the live synchronisation, the directory
          /home/user/remote_share/site_backup/ must contain bit-for-bit
          copies of the three *.conf files found in
          /home/user/source_configs/.

    Use concise, conventional commands. No root privileges are
    required.
truth:
<!--
        Initial filesystem layout before the agent starts
    -->
    Directory /home/user/source_configs/ exists with   0755 permissions
    and contains:

      /home/user/source_configs/router1.conf
         Content:
            hostname Router1
            interface Gig0/0/0
             ip address 10.0.1.1 255.255.255.0
            !

      /home/user/source_configs/router2.conf
         Content:
            hostname Router2
            interface Gig0/0/0
             ip address 10.0.2.1 255.255.255.0
            !

      /home/user/source_configs/router3.conf
         Content:
            hostname Router3
            interface Gig0/0/0
             ip address 10.0.3.1 255.255.255.0
            !

    Directory /home/user/remote_share/site_backup/ exists, permission
    0755, and is completely empty when the task begins.

    Directory /home/user/logs/ exists, permission 0755, and contains no
    files at task start.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    Expected artefacts AFTER correct completion:

    1. /home/user/logs/connectivity_test.log  
       • Will hold the full output from `ping -c 3 127.0.0.1` (exact
         wording depends on distro, so the test only checks that the
         file exists and contains the substrings
         "3 packets transmitted", "0% packet loss").

    2. /home/user/logs/rsync_dryrun.log  
       Exact byte-for-byte content (including trailing newlines):
            router1.conf\n
            router2.conf\n
            router3.conf\n

    3. /home/user/logs/rsync_run.log  
       Exact byte-for-byte content (including trailing newlines):
            router1.conf\n
            router2.conf\n
            router3.conf\n

    4. /home/user/remote_share/site_backup/  
       Contains the three *.conf files, each an exact copy (md5sum
       match) of the corresponding file in /home/user/source_configs/.
name: task_000000_20bf808c
